title: Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
note: The real CKA is a hands-on, performance-based exam (live cluster tasks), not multiple choice. These MCQ sets build conceptual recall and command knowledge — pair them with real kubectl practice on a cluster.

questions.stem: Which component stores all cluster state as the source of truth?

options.explanation: Correct. etcd is the consistent key-value store backing cluster state.
Wrong. The scheduler places pods; it stores nothing persistently.
Wrong. The kubelet runs pods on nodes.
Wrong. kube-proxy programs service networking.

questions.stem: Which component schedules pods onto nodes?

options.explanation: Correct. The scheduler assigns pods to suitable nodes.
Wrong. The kubelet runs assigned pods, it does not schedule them.
Wrong. etcd stores state.
Wrong. kube-proxy handles service traffic.

questions.stem: Which agent runs on each node and manages the pods there?

options.explanation: Correct. The kubelet ensures containers described in PodSpecs run.
Wrong. The API server is the control-plane front end.
Wrong. It runs control loops, not node pods.
Wrong. etcd stores state.

questions.stem: Which control-plane component exposes the Kubernetes API?

options.explanation: Correct. The API server is the front end for the control plane.
Wrong. It only schedules pods.
Wrong. The kubelet is a node agent.
Wrong. It handles service networking.

questions.stem: Which tool bootstraps a conformant cluster control plane and joins nodes?

options.explanation: Correct. kubeadm initializes the control plane and joins workers.
Wrong. kubectl is the API client, not a bootstrapper.
Wrong. The kubelet is the node agent.
Wrong. etcdctl manages etcd directly.

questions.stem: To back up cluster state, what should you snapshot?

options.explanation: Correct. Backing up etcd captures all cluster objects.
Wrong. That does not contain cluster object state.
Wrong. Those are derived, not the source of truth.
Wrong. The CNI is networking, not state storage.

questions.stem: Which object manages a stateless replicated set of pods with rolling updates?

options.explanation: Correct. Deployments manage ReplicaSets and rolling updates.
Wrong. DaemonSets run one pod per node.
Wrong. StatefulSets are for stable identity/storage.
Wrong. Jobs run to completion.

questions.stem: Which object ensures a copy of a pod runs on every (matching) node?

options.explanation: Correct. DaemonSets place one pod per eligible node.
Wrong. Deployments do not guarantee one-per-node.
Wrong. Jobs run to completion.
Wrong. ReplicaSets keep a count, not per-node placement.

questions.stem: Which object provides stable network identity and ordered, persistent storage?

options.explanation: Correct. StatefulSets give stable identities and per-pod volumes.
Wrong. Deployment pods are interchangeable.
Wrong. DaemonSets are per-node, not identity-stable sets.
Wrong. Jobs are run-to-completion.

questions.stem: Which mechanism repels pods from a node unless they tolerate it?

options.explanation: Correct. Taints repel pods lacking a matching toleration.
Wrong. nodeSelector attracts; it is set on the pod.
Wrong. Limits cap usage, not placement by condition.
Wrong. PDBs limit voluntary disruptions.

questions.stem: Which pod field requests that a pod land only on nodes with a given label?

options.explanation: Correct. nodeSelector constrains scheduling to labeled nodes.
Wrong. Taints are on nodes and repel pods.
Wrong. Tolerations allow scheduling onto tainted nodes.
Wrong. Limits cap usage, not target labels.

questions.stem: Which TWO actions prepare a node for maintenance safely?

options.explanation: Correct. cordon marks the node unschedulable.
Correct. drain evicts pods (respecting PDBs), skipping DaemonSets.
Wrong. Deleting removes the node object entirely.
Wrong. PreferNoSchedule is a soft hint, not a guarantee.

questions.stem: What does a readiness probe control?

options.text: Whether a pod receives traffic from Services
options.explanation: Correct. Failing readiness removes the pod from Service endpoints.
Wrong. That is the liveness probe.
Wrong. Probes run after scheduling.
Wrong. Probes do not control image pulls.

questions.stem: What does a liveness probe failure cause?

options.explanation: Correct. Liveness failures trigger a container restart.

options.text: The pod is removed from Service endpoints
options.explanation: Wrong. That is readiness.
Wrong. Probes do not cordon nodes.
Wrong. Probes do not scale workloads.

questions.stem: Which Service type exposes a pod on a static port across every node's IP?

options.explanation: Correct. NodePort opens the same port on all nodes.
Wrong. ClusterIP is internal-only.
Wrong. It maps to a DNS name via CNAME.
Wrong. Headless returns pod IPs directly.

questions.stem: Which Service type is the default and internal-only?

options.explanation: Correct. ClusterIP exposes the Service within the cluster only.
Wrong. NodePort exposes on nodes externally.
Wrong. LoadBalancer provisions an external LB.
Wrong. It is a DNS alias.

questions.stem: Which object routes external HTTP(S) traffic to Services by host/path rules?

options.explanation: Correct. Ingress provides L7 host/path routing to Services.
Wrong. NodePort is L4 per-node port exposure.
Wrong. ConfigMaps hold configuration data.
Wrong. PVs are storage, not routing.

questions.stem: What does a NetworkPolicy control?

options.explanation: Correct. NetworkPolicies restrict pod-to-pod traffic.
Wrong. That is the scheduler/affinity.
Wrong. Storage is unrelated to NetworkPolicy.
Wrong. That is liveness probes.

questions.stem: Which add-on provides pod networking (the CNI's role)?

options.text: A CNI plugin such as Calico or Cilium
options.explanation: Correct. CNI plugins implement the pod network.
Wrong. kube-proxy handles Services, not the pod network fabric.
Wrong. CoreDNS provides DNS, not the network fabric.
Wrong. etcd stores state.

questions.stem: In-cluster DNS for Services is typically provided by which component?

options.explanation: Correct. CoreDNS resolves Service and pod DNS names.
Wrong. The scheduler does not do DNS.
Wrong. etcd stores state, not DNS.
Wrong. The kubelet runs pods.

questions.stem: What does a PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) do?

options.text: Requests storage that binds to a PersistentVolume
options.explanation: Correct. A PVC is a user's request for storage.
Wrong. Images are set in the pod spec.
Wrong. That is a Service/Ingress.
Wrong. That is the scheduler.

questions.stem: A PVC stays Pending. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: No PV or StorageClass satisfies its size/access mode
options.explanation: Correct. Without a matching PV or dynamic provisioner the PVC stays Pending.
Wrong. Replica count does not affect binding.
Wrong. Network policy is unrelated to PVC binding.
Wrong. kube-proxy is service networking.

questions.stem: What does a StorageClass enable?

options.text: Dynamic provisioning of PersistentVolumes on demand
options.explanation: Correct. StorageClasses let PVs be created automatically.
Wrong. Storage classes are not networking.
Wrong. That is affinity/taints.
Wrong. Unrelated to storage classes.

questions.stem: Which access mode allows a volume to be mounted read-write by a single node?

options.explanation: Correct. RWO mounts read-write by one node.
Wrong. ROX is read-only by many nodes.
Wrong. RWX is read-write by many nodes.
Wrong. No such access mode.

questions.stem: What happens to a PV with reclaim policy Retain when its PVC is deleted?

options.text: The PV and its data are kept for manual handling
options.explanation: Correct. Retain preserves the volume and data after the claim is gone.
Wrong. That is the Delete policy.

options.text: The PV is recycled and wiped
options.explanation: Wrong. Recycle (deprecated) wipes; Retain keeps.
Wrong. Reclaim policy is about the volume, not pods.

questions.stem: A node shows NotReady. Which check comes first?

options.text: systemctl status kubelet on that node
options.explanation: Correct. NotReady usually traces to the kubelet.
Wrong. Deleting diagnoses nothing and is destructive.
Wrong. Unrelated to node readiness.
Wrong. That is remediation, not first diagnosis.

questions.stem: A pod is stuck in Pending. Which is a common cause?

options.text: Insufficient cluster resources or unschedulable constraints
options.explanation: Correct. Pending often means the scheduler cannot place the pod.
Wrong. That would be Completed, not Pending.
Wrong. Liveness affects running pods, not scheduling.
Wrong. Services do not block scheduling.

questions.stem: Which command shows recent events to help diagnose a failing pod?

options.explanation: Correct. describe surfaces events and state for the pod.
Wrong. That shows kubeconfig, not pod events.
Wrong. That lists resource types.
Wrong. That prints versions.

questions.stem: A pod shows CrashLoopBackOff. What does this indicate?

options.text: The container repeatedly starts and exits, so restarts are backing off
options.explanation: Correct. The kubelet delays restarts after repeated crashes.
Wrong. That is ImagePullBackOff.
Wrong. That is Pending.
Wrong. Unrelated to crash loops.

questions.stem: A pod shows ImagePullBackOff. What is the likely cause?

options.text: The image name/tag is wrong or registry auth is missing
options.explanation: Correct. Pull failures stem from bad references or missing credentials.
Wrong. That causes restarts, not pull errors.
Wrong. Storage issues differ from image pulls.
Wrong. Service networking is unrelated.

questions.stem: Which command streams logs from a running container?

options.explanation: Correct. logs -f follows container output.
Wrong. Not a valid form.
Wrong. describe shows events, not logs.
Wrong. exec runs commands, not log streaming.

questions.stem: What is the role of the controller-manager?

options.text: Runs control loops that drive actual state toward desired state
options.explanation: Correct. Controllers reconcile state continuously.
Wrong. etcd stores state.
Wrong. That is the scheduler.
Wrong. That is the CNI.

questions.stem: What does a HorizontalPodAutoscaler adjust?

options.text: The number of pod replicas based on metrics
options.explanation: Correct. The HPA scales replicas using metrics like CPU.

options.text: The CPU limit of a container
options.explanation: Wrong. That is a vertical concern.
Wrong. That is the cluster autoscaler.
Wrong. Unrelated to autoscaling.

questions.stem: Which field on a Service selects the pods it targets?

options.explanation: Correct. The selector matches pod labels to endpoints.
Wrong. That pins a pod to a node.
Wrong. That is the virtual IP, not selection.
Wrong. Affinity influences scheduling, not Service targets.

questions.stem: Which volume type injects key-value configuration into a pod?

options.explanation: Correct. ConfigMaps provide non-secret configuration.
Wrong. PVs are durable storage, not config.
Wrong. emptyDir is scratch space.
Wrong. hostPath mounts a node directory.

questions.stem: Which object stores sensitive data like passwords for pods?

options.explanation: Correct. Secrets hold sensitive data, base64-encoded by default.
Wrong. ConfigMaps are for non-sensitive config.
Wrong. PVs are storage volumes.
Wrong. SAs provide identity, not arbitrary secrets storage.

questions.stem: During a kubeadm upgrade, which component is typically upgraded first?

options.text: The control plane (kubeadm, then control-plane components)
options.explanation: Correct. Upgrade the control plane before worker nodes.
Wrong. Workers are upgraded after the control plane.
Wrong. CNI follows the documented order, not first.
Wrong. etcd may be upgraded as part of the process.

questions.stem: Which command checks node resource usage (requires metrics-server)?

options.explanation: Correct. top nodes shows CPU/memory usage via metrics-server.
Wrong. No such command.
Wrong. Not valid.
Wrong. Not a kubectl command.

questions.stem: What does a Job do?

options.text: Runs one or more pods to successful completion
options.explanation: Correct. Jobs run tasks to completion.
Wrong. That is a Deployment/long-running workload.
Wrong. That is a DaemonSet.
Wrong. That is a Service.

questions.stem: What does a CronJob do?

options.text: Creates Jobs on a time-based schedule
options.explanation: Correct. CronJobs schedule Jobs like cron.

options.text: Runs a Job once immediately only
options.explanation: Wrong. That is a plain Job.
Wrong. Unrelated.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: kube-proxy is responsible for what?

options.text: Implementing Service virtual IPs and routing to pod endpoints
options.explanation: Correct. kube-proxy programs node networking for Services.
Wrong. That is etcd.
Wrong. That is the scheduler.
Wrong. That is the kubelet/runtime.

questions.stem: Which storage option is ephemeral and shares the pod's lifecycle?

options.explanation: Correct. emptyDir lives and dies with the pod.
Wrong. PVs outlive pods.
Wrong. That provisions persistent storage.
Wrong. Secrets are config data, not scratch storage.

questions.stem: Which command applies a manifest declaratively?

options.explanation: Correct. apply reconciles resources to the manifest.
Wrong. run creates a single pod, not from -f in this sense.
Wrong. exec runs commands in a container.
Wrong. describe shows details, not applies.

questions.stem: Where does the kubelet get the desired pod configuration from?

options.explanation: Correct. The kubelet watches the API server for assigned pods.
Wrong. Components go through the API server, not etcd directly.
Wrong. kube-proxy handles Services.
Wrong. CoreDNS provides DNS.

questions.stem: Which field guarantees a minimum number of pods stay available during voluntary disruptions?

options.explanation: Correct. PDBs cap voluntary disruptions to protect availability.
Wrong. Requests reserve resources, not availability.
Wrong. That constrains placement.
Wrong. Probes gate traffic, not disruption budgets.

questions.stem: What DNS name format resolves a Service in another namespace?

options.explanation: Correct. That fully qualified name resolves cross-namespace.
Wrong. Not a valid DNS format.
Wrong. That is a path, not DNS.
Wrong. Missing namespace and svc segments.

questions.stem: Which field in a pod mounts a volume into a container?

options.explanation: Correct. volumeMounts attach a declared volume into the container.
Wrong. PVCs are referenced under volumes, not as the mount field.
Wrong. No such field.
Wrong. No such field.

questions.stem: Two pods cannot reach each other though both are Running. What is a likely culprit?

options.text: A NetworkPolicy is blocking the traffic
options.explanation: Correct. Restrictive NetworkPolicies can deny pod-to-pod traffic.
Wrong. That restarts containers, it does not block peers.
Wrong. Storage is unrelated to pod connectivity.
Wrong. Storage classes do not affect networking.

questions.stem: Which command lists all nodes and their status?

options.explanation: Correct. get nodes lists nodes and Ready status.
Wrong. That lists pods, not node status.
Wrong. That dumps debug info, not a node list.
Wrong. No such resource.

questions.stem: Which component must agree with the Service's selector for traffic to flow to a pod?

options.explanation: Correct. A Service routes to pods whose labels match its selector.
Wrong. Node name does not affect Service selection.
Wrong. Restart policy is unrelated to selection.
Wrong. Storage is unrelated to Service selection.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of a namespace?

options.text: To logically partition cluster resources for isolation and quotas
options.explanation: Correct. Namespaces scope and isolate resources.

options.text: To assign pods to specific nodes
options.explanation: Wrong. That is scheduling/affinity.
Wrong. That is the CNI.
Wrong. Namespaces hold many resource types.

questions.stem: Which file does kubectl use to find cluster connection details?

options.explanation: Correct. kubeconfig holds clusters, users, and contexts.
Wrong. That configures the node agent.
Wrong. No such standard kubectl file.
Wrong. That configures networking.

questions.stem: Which RBAC objects grant namespaced permissions to a subject?

options.explanation: Correct. Role defines permissions; RoleBinding grants them in a namespace.
Wrong. A Namespace is not a binding object.
Wrong. Those provide identity/credentials, not permission grants.
Wrong. Those are different controls.

questions.stem: Which RBAC object grants permissions cluster-wide or across namespaces?

options.explanation: Correct. ClusterRoles apply cluster-wide when bound with a ClusterRoleBinding.
Wrong. Role is namespaced only.
Wrong. Namespaces are not RBAC grants.
Wrong. ConfigMaps hold config data.

questions.stem: What does a ServiceAccount provide?

options.text: An identity for processes running in pods to call the API
options.explanation: Correct. ServiceAccounts authenticate in-cluster workloads.
Wrong. That is PVs/PVCs.
Wrong. That is affinity/taints.
Wrong. That is Services/kube-proxy.

questions.stem: How do you scale a Deployment to 5 replicas imperatively?

options.explanation: Correct. scale sets the desired replica count.
Wrong. No resize subcommand.
Wrong. Not the correct form.
Wrong. apply takes a manifest, not that flag.

questions.stem: What does kubectl rollout undo do?

options.text: Reverts a Deployment to a previous revision
options.explanation: Correct. rollout undo rolls back to a prior ReplicaSet.
Wrong. It reverts, not deletes.
Wrong. That is rollout pause.
Wrong. That is a scale operation.

questions.stem: Which strategy updates pods gradually to avoid downtime?

options.explanation: Correct. RollingUpdate replaces pods incrementally.
Wrong. Recreate terminates all old pods first, causing downtime.

options.text: Canary is a built-in strategy field
options.explanation: Wrong. Canary is a pattern, not a strategy enum value.

options.text: BlueGreen is a built-in strategy field
options.explanation: Wrong. Not a built-in Deployment strategy.

questions.stem: Which scheduling feature co-locates or spreads pods relative to other pods?

options.explanation: Correct. Pod (anti-)affinity places pods relative to others.
Wrong. Taints repel based on node conditions.
Wrong. Limits cap usage.
Wrong. PDBs manage disruptions.

questions.stem: What does a resource request influence?

options.text: Scheduling decisions and guaranteed minimum resources
options.explanation: Correct. Requests reserve capacity and guide the scheduler.

options.text: The maximum a container may use
options.explanation: Wrong. That is a limit.
Wrong. Unrelated.
Wrong. Unrelated to requests.

questions.stem: Which Service type provisions an external cloud load balancer?

options.explanation: Correct. LoadBalancer requests an external LB from the cloud.
Wrong. ClusterIP is internal-only.
Wrong. NodePort exposes node ports, not a cloud LB.
Wrong. Headless returns pod IPs.

questions.stem: What is a headless Service (clusterIP: None) used for?

options.text: Returning individual pod IPs, often for StatefulSets
options.explanation: Correct. Headless Services expose pod IPs directly via DNS.
Wrong. That is LoadBalancer.
Wrong. That is a NetworkPolicy concern.
Wrong. That is a ConfigMap.

questions.stem: Which controller must be running for Ingress resources to take effect?

options.explanation: Correct. Ingress objects need a controller to fulfill them.
Wrong. The scheduler does not handle Ingress.
Wrong. etcd stores state.
Wrong. That serves metrics, not Ingress.

questions.stem: A NodePort Service uses which default port range?

options.explanation: Correct. NodePort defaults to the 30000–32767 range.
Wrong. That is the privileged range, not NodePort.
Wrong. Not the NodePort default.
Wrong. Outside the default range.

questions.stem: Which command shows PersistentVolumes in the cluster?

options.explanation: Correct. get pv lists PersistentVolumes.
Wrong. Not a valid resource name.
Wrong. Not a resource.
Wrong. Not a Kubernetes resource.

questions.stem: Which field links a pod to a PVC?

options.explanation: Correct. Pods reference a PVC by claimName under volumes.
Wrong. StorageClass is on the PVC, not the container.
Wrong. Services do not reference PVCs.
Wrong. Nodes do not claim PVCs.

questions.stem: What does the Delete reclaim policy do when a PVC is removed?

options.text: Deletes the PV and its backing storage
options.explanation: Correct. Delete removes the volume and underlying storage.

options.text: Keeps the PV for manual reuse
options.explanation: Wrong. That is Retain.
Wrong. Reclaim policy concerns volumes, not pods.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: Which access mode allows read-write mounting by many nodes simultaneously?

options.explanation: Correct. RWX supports concurrent read-write across nodes.
Wrong. RWO is single-node read-write.
Wrong. ROX is read-only by many.
Wrong. That restricts to a single pod, not many nodes.

questions.stem: Which command runs a shell inside a running pod's container?

options.explanation: Correct. exec -it opens an interactive shell.
Wrong. run creates a new pod, it does not exec into one.
Wrong. Not the standard exec form.
Wrong. No such subcommand.

questions.stem: kubectl get pods shows a pod in Terminating for a long time. A common cause is?

options.text: A finalizer or stuck volume detach preventing deletion
options.explanation: Correct. Finalizers or detach issues can stall termination.
Wrong. Pending and Terminating are different phases.
Wrong. That is start-up, not termination.
Wrong. Service deletion does not hold pods in Terminating.

questions.stem: Which command helps diagnose API server connectivity from kubectl?

options.explanation: Correct. cluster-info reports control-plane endpoints.
Wrong. Not a valid form.
Wrong. Not a valid command.
Wrong. Not a resource.

questions.stem: Static pods are managed by which component, not the scheduler?

options.text: The kubelet (from a manifest path on the node)
options.explanation: Correct. The kubelet runs static pods directly from manifests.
Wrong. Static pods bypass the scheduler.
Wrong. etcd stores state.
Wrong. kube-proxy handles Services.

questions.stem: Where are static pod manifests typically located on a kubeadm node?

options.explanation: Correct. The kubelet watches that directory for static pods.
Wrong. That holds pod runtime data, not manifests.
Wrong. That is CNI config.
Wrong. That holds logs.

questions.stem: Which command drains a node while respecting PodDisruptionBudgets?

options.explanation: Correct. drain evicts pods honoring PDBs.
Wrong. That removes the node object without graceful eviction.
Wrong. cordon stops new scheduling but does not evict.
Wrong. Tainting alone does not drain.

questions.stem: After draining and maintaining a node, how do you allow scheduling again?

options.explanation: Correct. uncordon makes the node schedulable again.
Wrong. drain evicts pods; it does not re-enable scheduling.
Wrong. That removes the node.
Wrong. That removes a taint, not the cordon.

questions.stem: What does kubectl rollout status deployment/<name> show?

options.text: Whether the rollout has completed successfully
options.explanation: Correct. rollout status reports progress/completion.
Wrong. That is kubectl logs.
Wrong. That is get nodes.
Wrong. That is get endpoints.

questions.stem: Which object lets you run a pod with elevated, time-bound debugging tools attached to a running pod?

options.explanation: Correct. kubectl debug attaches ephemeral containers for troubleshooting.
Wrong. That does not attach to an existing pod.
Wrong. DaemonSets run per node, not attached to a pod.
Wrong. Jobs run separate pods to completion.

questions.stem: Which resource defines a set of endpoints for a Service automatically?

options.explanation: Correct. EndpointSlices track the pod endpoints for a Service.
Wrong. ConfigMaps hold config, not endpoints.
Wrong. PVs are storage.
Wrong. Quotas limit usage.

questions.stem: Pods can reach external internet but not each other across nodes. What should you check first?

options.text: The CNI plugin / pod network configuration
options.explanation: Correct. Cross-node pod traffic depends on a healthy CNI.
Wrong. Replicas do not affect connectivity.
Wrong. Storage is unrelated.
Wrong. Probes gate Service traffic, not raw pod-to-pod routing.

questions.stem: Which command shows PVCs and their bound status?

options.explanation: Correct. get pvc lists claims and binding status.
Wrong. Not a valid resource name.
Wrong. That shows the class, not claims.
Wrong. Not a valid resource.

questions.stem: What does the ReadWriteOncePod access mode guarantee?

options.text: The volume is mounted read-write by a single pod only
options.explanation: Correct. RWOP restricts access to exactly one pod.
Wrong. That is ReadWriteMany.
Wrong. That is ReadOnlyMany.
Wrong. RWOP allows exactly one pod.

questions.stem: Which command lists pods across all namespaces?

options.explanation: Correct. -A (or --all-namespaces) lists pods everywhere.
Wrong. No such flag.
Wrong. No such flag for all namespaces.
Wrong. Not valid syntax.

questions.stem: A container is OOMKilled. What does this mean?

options.text: It exceeded its memory limit and was terminated
options.explanation: Correct. OOMKilled means the container hit its memory cap.
Wrong. That is ImagePullBackOff.
Wrong. That removes it from endpoints.

options.text: The node ran out of disk
options.explanation: Wrong. That is disk pressure, not OOMKilled.

questions.stem: kubectl reports 'Unable to connect to the server'. Which is the most likely first check?

options.text: The kubeconfig context and API server endpoint
options.explanation: Correct. Connection errors often stem from wrong context/endpoint.
Wrong. Probes do not affect kubectl connectivity.
Wrong. Storage is unrelated to API connectivity.
Wrong. Unrelated to control-plane reachability.

questions.stem: What does kubectl config use-context do?

options.explanation: Correct. use-context changes which context kubectl targets.
Wrong. It only switches context.
Wrong. Unrelated.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: Which probe delays liveness/readiness checks until an app has started?

options.explanation: Correct. The startup probe protects slow-starting apps.
Wrong. Liveness restarts unhealthy containers.
Wrong. Readiness gates traffic.
Wrong. No such probe.

questions.stem: What runs before app containers and must complete successfully first?

options.explanation: Correct. Init containers run to completion before app containers start.
Wrong. Sidecars run alongside, not strictly before.
Wrong. Those are for debugging running pods.
Wrong. Unrelated concept.

questions.stem: Which object limits aggregate resource consumption in a namespace?

options.explanation: Correct. ResourceQuotas cap total usage per namespace.
Wrong. LimitRange sets defaults/bounds, not namespace totals.
Wrong. That governs traffic.
Wrong. That influences scheduling priority.

questions.stem: What does a LimitRange do?

options.text: Sets default and min/max resource constraints for pods/containers in a namespace
options.explanation: Correct. LimitRange enforces per-object resource bounds.
Wrong. That is ResourceQuota.
Wrong. That is Ingress.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: Which controller dynamically provisions volumes for matching PVCs?

options.text: The CSI driver / external provisioner for the StorageClass
options.explanation: Correct. CSI provisioners create PVs on demand.
Wrong. That handles Services.
Wrong. That handles DNS.
Wrong. It places pods, not volumes.

questions.stem: Which object expands a PVC's size when the StorageClass allows it?

options.text: Editing the PVC's requested storage with allowVolumeExpansion enabled
options.explanation: Correct. Volume expansion requires allowVolumeExpansion on the class.
Wrong. Pod recreation does not resize the volume.
Wrong. Nodes do not resize PVCs.
Wrong. Unrelated.

questions.stem: Which command shows resource usage of pods (with metrics-server)?

options.explanation: Correct. top pods reports per-pod CPU/memory.
Wrong. No such command.
Wrong. Not valid.
Wrong. Not valid.

questions.stem: The scheduler leaves a pod Pending with 'Insufficient cpu'. What resolves it?

options.text: Free capacity, lower the request, or add nodes
options.explanation: Correct. The pod needs schedulable CPU to be placed.
Wrong. Probes do not affect scheduling capacity.
Wrong. Services do not provide CPU.

options.text: Delete the namespace quota only blindly
options.explanation: Wrong. Quota may not be the cause; capacity is.

questions.stem: Which command outputs a resource definition you can edit and re-apply?

options.explanation: Correct. -o yaml emits the manifest for editing.
Wrong. describe is human-readable, not re-appliable YAML.
Wrong. logs shows container output.
Wrong. top shows metrics.

questions.stem: Which field sets the container image for a pod?

options.explanation: Correct. The image field names the container image.
Wrong. image lives under containers, not the pod spec root.
Wrong. metadata holds names/labels, not images.
Wrong. status is read-only state.

questions.stem: Which command shows the endpoints backing a Service?

options.explanation: Correct. get endpoints lists the pod IPs behind a Service.
Wrong. routes is not a core resource.
Wrong. Not a resource.
Wrong. Ingress is separate from Service endpoints.

questions.stem: Which command shows storage classes?

options.explanation: Correct. get storageclass lists available classes.
Wrong. Not valid.
Wrong. Not a resource.
Wrong. Not a valid resource name.

questions.stem: Which approach gives a quick health view of all control-plane pods on a kubeadm cluster?

options.explanation: Correct. Control-plane components run as pods in kube-system.
Wrong. Control-plane pods are in kube-system.
Wrong. That shows usage, not component health.
Wrong. No such resource.

questions.stem: What does kubectl apply --dry-run=client do?

options.text: Validates and renders the manifest without sending it to the server
options.explanation: Correct. Client dry-run previews changes locally without applying.

options.text: Applies the change but skips logging
options.explanation: Wrong. Dry-run does not apply changes.
Wrong. Dry-run does not delete.
Wrong. Unrelated to dry-run.

questions.stem: Which field controls how a pod responds to restarts after completion or failure?

options.explanation: Correct. restartPolicy (Always/OnFailure/Never) governs restarts.
Wrong. That selects the scheduler, not restart behavior.
Wrong. That sets scheduling priority.
Wrong. That sets identity.
